Now, not necessarily verbatim. It can be shown rather than said, but absolutely we need to take control of them by arriving larger than life and lording a presentation of ourselves over them, rubbing their noses in it that is greater than the most successful of any of them. So like I have a client right now who is selling coaching to a peer group and in that peer group he is I think number four or number five top earner, but there are three above him and there are some others pretty close to him. But we need him to be in this position with all of them. Fortunately he is in a really small town in a terrible market where nobody would think you could make any money. So even though somebody is out producing him they are doing it in a good market. Bingo. Gives me the fact that he is in this rat hole. Now I also have to explain away why he is living in a rat hole place, but so I buy a problem when I buy the asset. He also has to drive a Bentley, none of the rest of them got a Bentley while he and Frank Kern went out and got drunk and Frank Kern got him to buy a Bentley. But now that we got it, by God we are going to use it. And so if you looked at his stuff pre-Dan it was all heavily weighted towards features, benefits, hears, methodology. It is increasingly weighted towards I am your trainer, you are the trainee. My life is fabulous, yours is okay. And it is in their face. If you look at page 174, Jim Blanchard, by the way this is a guy we are studying for those of you in the newsletter business, those of you in the information marketing business. There is a book, it is out of print. I will try to remember to bring it tomorrow so you can try and find it. I know where it is, I just forgot to throw it in the box. Harrison gave it to me, I have not seen it in years. So Blanchard was the big guy in the Goldbug business during the Jimmy Carter recession. And spawned most of the guys you see, Hawking Gold today, the whole hard money movement. And what is important about the piece on Blanchard, so this is a piece out of a newsletter pitch on 174, is really the first column on the left hand side beginning with the first paragraph and it makes him a larger than life figure. If you flip over to 176 and 177, there is three categories of things that need to be present. Humble beginnings. What happens if you don't have them? You are at a big disadvantage. So it would be better if you had them. Because most of the people you are going to sell to have humble beginnings. So humble beginnings. I tell, when I am talking to information marketing beginners, you probably heard me tell it, I tell the cat who licks stamp story. So how many of you know the cat who licks stamp story? Okay, good, almost all of you. So I won't do the long version, I will just remind. Which happens to be true. So the cat who licks, it has the added virtue of truth. Well not all stories do. And I do consider it an added virtue. So the cat who licks stamp story is for mail order beginners who think that they are going to need a lot of resources in order to get started in info marketing. So the cat who licks stamp story is simply about me doing my early mail order business right after my first divorce. And she left me a spoon, a fork, a knife, a plate, a cup and her damned cat. And that's it. And the cat hated me by the way. But after about eight days it figured out the meal ticket was gone and so it had to at least tolerate me. And so the cat's job was to lick the envelope and lick the stamp. And it turns out they are pretty good at it as long as you give them a little bowl of water so they can. And I'm sure it shortens their life but I didn't like the cat either. And they got nine so what's the big deal right? So literally the cat would sit on the coffee table. I would have TV on and I'd have my mailings for the night stretched out and the cat would sit facing me and it had to do its work before it got fed so it got it right. And you would hold out the strip of stamps and the cat would go and then you would put the stamps on. And then it would freshen its tongue in a little bowl of water and then you would do it again right. And so really all you need right is a sales letter, a list, and a cat who will lick the stamps and you're good to go right. Now the reason I tell it right is to get over the I'm going to need a bunch of resources that I don't have right. And so it's reassuring to a complete novice who feels overwhelmed. It's a humble beginning story. Now again it actually happens to have the virtue of truth. But if it didn't I'd have to invent it or find some other story that could be used for that purpose. That's category of stuff number one. You need when you do larger than life. You need humble beginnings because otherwise you can wind up doing larger than life. And this kind of gets to where you were Dave. You can wind up doing larger than life and just be perceived as an arrogant egotistical asshole without the humble beginning story. So what proof of importance is if you're going to be larger than life other people but you have to share that opinion. Otherwise again you're just an arrogant asshole with a Bentley. So there has to be proof that other people believe you're a big deal. And you got to show the proof. So there's a whole category of how do you show that. Big audiences is one. Hopefully Turner Turner Turner gets here and we can show it to you. So I just showed you a couple of Turner things.
